rag_rules: Insert generated AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, for example `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>` in contracts
generic_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for component type contracts and implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>` in contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the properties
Do not use AADL property names as AGREE variables
